title: cytisus hirsutus subsp. leucotrichus
intro: Cytisus hirsutus subsp. leucotrichus, also known as the white hairy broom, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Cytisus hirsutus subsp. leucotrichus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and exhibit immunomodulatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
